mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations, directly suppressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >65% of months where FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.25 percentage points signals labor market deterioration and impending corporate earnings declines, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >70% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in >60% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power (t-stat < 1.5) in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Short-term returns are negative in >55% of months with rising INDPRO, or the signal's predictive accuracy drops below 52% in recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth falling below 2.0 percent indicates demand contraction and reduced corporate profit margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ growth crosses below 2.0% threshold
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >65% of low-growth quarters, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions (F-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative returns in >60% of observations, or the interaction effect is statistically insignificant in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production signals expanding economic capacity and higher corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year window, or 60-day forward returns show no directional bias during production expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month CPI inflation exceeding 0.3 percent triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Short-term returns remain positive in >70% of high-inflation months, or the signal fails to predict direction with p < 0.05 in regime-specific subsamples.
